Query: Recent publications in top rehabilitation journals on telerehabilitation efficacy
Response A:
Based solely on the provided Context, recent publications in top rehabilitation journals indicate that telerehabilitation is effective for several conditions.

For patients after total joint arthroplasty, internet-based telerehabilitation shows comparable improvement to face-to-face rehabilitation in pain relief, range of motion, physical function, and health-related quality of life, with high patient satisfaction (Doc 3). For stroke patients, virtual reality-based telerehabilitation demonstrates comparable outcomes to conventional in-person rehabilitation for upper extremity function and balance function (Doc 2). The evidence also supports integrating telerehabilitation with traditional interventions after hip and knee prosthesis surgery to accelerate efficiency in existing healthcare systems (Doc 1).
